# Signality - Signal-First Utilities for Angular

Signality is a comprehensive library of signal-first utilities for Angular. SSR-ready, type-safe, and designed for seamless reactive composition with DI-interop.

## Core Information

- **Homepage**: https://signality.dev
- **GitHub**: https://github.com/signality/signality
- **npm**: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@signality/core
- **Documentation**: https://signality.dev

## Packages

### @signality/core
Core utilities: browser APIs, element utilities, reactive helpers, router and forms integration
- Installation: `npm install @signality/core`
- Documentation: https://signality.dev/browser/battery

### @signality/cdk-interop
CDK integration: focus monitoring, interactivity checking, and input modality detection
- Installation: `npm install @signality/cdk-interop @angular/cdk`
- Documentation: https://signality.dev/cdk-interop/focus-monitor
- Requires: `@signality/core` and `@angular/cdk` as peer dependencies

## Key Concepts

### Signal-First Design
Every utility is reactive by default. Changes propagate automatically through your application. No manual subscriptions needed.

### MaybeSignal<T>
Most utility parameters accept `MaybeSignal<T>`, which is a union type: `T | Signal<T>`. This means you can pass either a static value or a signal.

### WithInjector Pattern
All options interfaces extend `WithInjector`, allowing you to pass an `injector` option when using utilities outside of Angular's injection context.

### SSR Compatibility
All Signality utilities are safe for Server-Side Rendering (SSR). Browser-only APIs are guarded and return safe defaults on the server.

## Browser Utilities

- `battery()` - Reactive battery status and charging state
- `clipboard()` - Reactive clipboard read/write operations
- `geolocation()` - Reactive geolocation tracking
- `pageVisibility()` - Reactive page visibility state
- `storage()` - Reactive localStorage/sessionStorage with sync
- `network()` - Reactive network connection status
- `fullscreen()` - Reactive fullscreen API
- And many more...

## Element Utilities

- `elementSize()` - Reactive element dimensions
- `elementVisibility()` - Reactive Intersection Observer
- `dropzone()` - Reactive drag & drop zone
- `windowSize()` - Reactive window dimensions
- `scroll()` - Reactive scroll position
- And many more...

## Router Utilities (included in @signality/core)

- `params()` - Reactive route parameters with generics support
- `queryParams()` - Reactive query parameters with transform functions
- `fragment()` - Reactive URL fragment tracking
- `title()` - Reactive route title tracking
- `url()` - Reactive current URL with absolute URL option
- `routeData()` - Reactive route data from resolvers or static configuration
- `routerListener()` - Event-driven listener for router events with type-safe callbacks

## Forms Utilities (included in @signality/core)

- `cva()` - Reactive wrapper around Angular's Control Value Accessor (CVA) pattern

## CDK Interop Utilities

- `focusMonitor()` - Reactive focus tracking with origin detection
- `inputModality()` - Reactive input method detection
- `liveAnnouncer()` - Reactive screen reader announcements with message tracking

## Utils

- `debounced()` - Debounced writable signal
- `throttled()` - Throttled writable signal

## Framework Compatibility

- **Angular**: v19.0.0+
- **TypeScript**: 5.4+

## Engineering Rules

- All utilities MUST be implemented using the `setupContext` helper function
- SSR support is mandatory - use `isServer` checks for browser-only APIs
- Always provide strict TypeScript types - no `any`
- Use signals for state management
- Automatic cleanup via `DestroyRef` for all resources
- Follow naming conventions: `*Options` for config, `*Ref` for return types, lowercase for functions
